story: As an Active Recorder, I want to reconcile an account by entering its actual real-world balance as of a specific date and time, so that I can detect and correct discrepancies while correctly accounting for transactions that occurred after the check.
acceptance_criteria: User can initiate a reconciliation for any account and select the date/time of the real-world balance check (pre-filled with current system time)
The app displays the account's recorded balance *as of the selected date/time* alongside a field to enter the actual verified balance
The app shows the discrepancy amount (actual minus recorded at that time)
If discrepancy is zero, the account is marked as reconciled at that date/time, no transaction is created
For Bank, Cash, and Credit Card accounts: if a discrepancy exists, user can create a correction transaction dated at the reconciliation time
Transactions dated *after* the reconciliation time are ignored during the balance check but preserved in the ledger
For Investment accounts: if a discrepancy exists, user can create a Revaluation transaction to record the change in asset value at that time
All correction transactions are clearly labeled and distinct from manual entries in the timeline
